full_prompt: For an 8-hour modelling-only engineering-science benchmark, submit an analysis-driven preliminary design of a 20 m single-span pedestrian footbridge over a river crossing on a city council pedestrian network, carrying a 3 m wide deck. The bridge is a simply-supported steel Warren truss with top and bottom chords in AS/NZS 3679.1 Grade 300 steel (Young's modulus 200 GPa, Poisson 0.30, yield 300 MPa, density 7850 kg/m^3) and timber deck planking (LVL, Young's modulus 13 GPa, design tension parallel to grain 22 MPa, density 600 kg/m^3). Design loads per AS/NZS 1170.1 pedestrian crowd: (LC1) uniformly distributed pedestrian live load 5 kPa on the full 20 m x 3 m deck; (LC2) concentrated load 10 kN on a 0.5 m x 0.5 m patch at mid-span deck; (LC3) pedestrian dynamic vertical: 280 N at first-mode frequency 2 Hz applied at mid-span to check serviceability; (LC4) wind lateral on elevation: 1.0 kPa on the full truss side elevation per AS/NZS 1170.2 region A7 ultimate. Pass/fail: (R1) max truss chord axial stress under LC1+LC2 below 200 MPa (yield 300 / 1.5), (R2) mid-span vertical deflection under LC1 below L/500 = 40 mm, (R3) vertical acceleration under LC3 below 0.5 m/s^2 per AS 5100.2 pedestrian comfort, (R4) lateral displacement under LC4 below L/400 = 50 mm. Because organizers mark the analysis narrative rather than a 3D geometry, the deliverable is an 8-page analytical report; we include a representative STEP AP242 skeleton model so that the schema remains uniform with rest of the benchmark set.
short_prompt:
For an 8-hour modelling-only engineering-science exercise, submit a preliminary design of a 20 m single-span pedestrian footbridge carrying a 3 m wide deck over a city-network river crossing. The bridge is a simply-supported steel Warren truss (AS/NZS 3679.1 Grade 300 class chords) with an LVL timber deck. Design loads per AS/NZS 1170.1 (pedestrian live), AS/NZS 1170.2 (wind, region A7 ULS), and AS 5100.2 pedestrian-comfort serviceability.

Note: the brief is analysis-narrative-driven; a representative STEP skeleton is expected for schema uniformity, not detailed geometry.

Load cases.
- LC1 pedestrian UDL: 5 kPa over the full 20 m x 3 m deck.
- LC2 concentrated: 10 kN on a 0.5 x 0.5 m patch at mid-span.
- LC3 pedestrian dynamic vertical: 280 N at 2 Hz (first mode) at mid-span.
- LC4 wind lateral: 1.0 kPa on the full side elevation per AS/NZS 1170.2 A7 ULS.

prompt.title: Engineering-science pedestrian bridge model
prompt.brief: 20 m Warren-truss pedestrian bridge sized to AS/NZS 1170 and AS 5100 serviceability.
